channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Chesterfield Grove (Along wall of Joe the Juice)-Flytipping-5th Complaint, 20th May
item.description:
5th Complaint: It is completely unacceptable that this is a site for dumping/non-removal of a toilet tank lid and nothing is being done about its removal and it has now encouraged more flytipping and inappropriate disposal. The fact that it is a street full of young children and the health and safety is dismissed is shocking. The Duty of Care is not being demonstrated of not enforcing the law towards the organisation or residence in regards to the responsible manner of rubbish disposal/removal, where it will encourage unhygienic practices.This sends a message that this conduct has no accountability. Health and safety of the residents and wider community should be paramount, not ignored.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Chesterfield Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 59 Lordship Lane-Flytipping-4th Complaint, 20th May
item.description:
4th complaint: Wooden boards flytipped. They should not be on the pavement as it endangers pedestrians. It should be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 59, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chesterfield Grove (next to car wash)-Bin overflowing with wast. 2nd Complaint, 19th May
item.description:

item.title: Uncollected rubbish and fly tipping, 19th May
item.description:
A bin that has been left by the council for months has been left uncollected. People are using it as a dumping site. Also multiple trolleys
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salutation Road, Greenwich

item.title: Illegally parking on double yellow every single day, 19th May
item.description:
Vehicle illegally parking every single day from around 8:30 - 9:00 pm till the following morning at the entrance of 17 Stanway Street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hoxton Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water bubbling up and pouring down Rafford Way, 19th May
item.description:
Please can this water issue be addressed. It’s bubbling up from under these two inspection covers and pouring down Rafford Way towards The Chase.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rafford Way, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped shopping trolley, 19th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rafford Way, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Small pile of leaves/cuttings, 19th May
item.description:
Please can this small pile of cuttings be cleared away. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Antisocial revving of loud bike engine, 19th May
item.description:
Someone on the Park View Estate regularly revs the unsilenced engine of their motorbike late at night before riding it around the surrounding roads. The noise is unacceptable and can be heard by residents of all the roads around. It’s just not necessary to rev a bike like that and if a legal silencer was fitted it wouldn’t be this loud. Can someone find out who this is and have a word with them please?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park View, Highbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
